metadata.description: Deployment: Declarative Pod Updates. Rolling Update/Rollback. HPA for Horizontal Scaling. ### Explanation: - **Declarative Pod Updates**: This refers to a method where the desired state of the application is defined, and Kubernetes automatically handles the updates to achieve that state. ```python # Example: Define a deployment with a specific image version import kubernetes.client as k8s_client app_deployment = k8s_client.V1Deployment( metadata=k8s_client.V1ObjectMeta(name="my-app"), spec=k8s_client.V1DeploymentSpec( replicas=3, template=k8s_client.V1PodTemplateSpec( metadata=k8s_client.V1ObjectMeta(labels={"app": "my-app"}), spec=k8s_client.V1PodSpec(containers=[ k8s_client.V1Container(name="my-container", image="nginx:latest") ]) ) ) ) # Apply the deployment api_instance = k8s_client.AppsV1Api() api_response = api_instance.create_namespaced_deployment(body=app_deployment, namespace="default") print("Deployment created. status='%s'" % str(api_response.status)) ``` - **Rolling Update/Rollback**: This is a strategy where Kubernetes updates the application by gradually replacing old pods with new ones to ensure minimal downtime. ```python # Example: Rolling update for a deployment api_instance.patch_namespaced_deployment( name="my-app", namespace="default", body={ "spec": { "template": { "metadata": { "annotations": { "kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration": '{"spec":{"strategy":{"type":"RollingUpdate","rollingUpdate":{"maxUnavailable":1}}}}' } } } } } ) ``` - **HPA for Horizontal Scaling**: This stands for Horizontal Pod Autoscaler, which automatically scales the number of pods based on observed CPU utilization or other metrics. ```python # Example: Create an HPA to scale a deployment based on CPU usage hpa = k8s_client.V1HorizontalPodAutoscaler( metadata=k8s_client.V1ObjectMeta(name="my-app-hpa"), spec=k8s_client.V1HorizontalPodAutoscalerSpec( scale_target_ref=k8s_client.V1CrossVersionObjectReference( api_version="apps/v1", kind="Deployment", name="my-app" ), min_replicas=1, max_replicas=10, target_cpu_utilization_percentage=50 ) ) # Apply the HPA hpa_api_instance = k8s_client.AutoscalingV2Api() api_response = hpa_api_instance.create_namespaced_horizontal_pod_autoscaler(body=hpa, namespace="default") print("HPA created. status='%s'" % str(api_response.status)) ```

data_pointers.content_preview: Deployment: Declarative Pod Updates. Rolling Update/Rollback. HPA for Horizontal Scaling. ### Explanation: - **Declarative Pod Updates**: This refers to a method where the desired state of the application is defined, and Kubernetes automatically handles the updates to achieve that state. ```python # Example: Define a deployment with a specific image version import kubernetes.client as k8s_client app_deployment = k8s_client.V1Deployment( metadata=k8s_client.V1ObjectMeta(name="my-app"), spec=k8s_client.V1DeploymentSpec( replicas=3, template=k8s_client.V1PodTemplateSpec( metadata=k8s_client.V1ObjectMeta(labels={"app": "my-app"}), spec=k8s_client.V1PodSpec(containers=[ k8s_client.V1Container(name="my-container", image="nginx:latest") ]) ) ) ) # Apply the deployment api_instance = k8s_client.AppsV1Api() api_response = api_instance.create_namespaced_deployment(body=app_deployment, namespace="default") print("Deployment created. status='%s'" % str(api_response.status)) ``` - **Rolling Update/Rollback**: This is a strategy where Kubernetes updates the application by gradually replacing old pods with new ones to ensure minimal downtime. ```python # Example: Rolling update for a deployment api_instance.patch_namespaced_deployment( name="my-app", namespace="default", body={ "spec": { "template": { "metadata": { "annotations": { "kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration": '{"spec":{"strategy":{"type":"RollingUpdate","rollingUpdate":{"maxUnavailable":1}}}}' } } } } } ) ``` - **HPA for Horizontal Scaling**: This stands for Horizontal Pod Autoscaler, which automatically scales the number of pods based on observed CPU utilization or other metrics. ```python # Example: Create an HPA to scale a deployment based on CPU usage hpa = k8s_client.V1HorizontalPodAutoscaler( metadata=k8s_client.V1ObjectMeta(name="my-app-hpa"), spec=k8s_client.V1HorizontalPodAutoscalerSpec( scale_target_ref=k8s_client.V1CrossVersionObjectReference( api_version="apps/v1", kind="Deployment", name="my-app" ), min_replicas=1, max_replicas=10, target_cpu_utilization_percentage=50 ) ) # Apply the HPA hpa_api_instance = k8s_client.AutoscalingV2Api() api_response = hpa_api_instance.create_namespaced_horizontal_pod_autoscaler(body=hpa, namespace="default") print("HPA created. status='%s'" % str(api_response.status)) ```
